title: phebalium squameum
intro: Phebalium squameum is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Western Australia and a member of the citrus family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving cold symptoms, and supporting digestion. In herbal preparations, Phebalium squameum is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
